scenario: Atlas Telco handles 9000 customer requests per day through Camunda 8.8 Forms. A Select field on the support form must show 12 language options that are already maintained by the back office as a process variable named languageData, so the team avoids redeploying the form whenever the catalogue changes within 30 minutes.
question: Which property of the form field references the process variable that supplies the options array?

options.text: The Static options items list, which is populated row by row in the form designer and is the only documented way to feed values into a Select field
The Default value property of the Select field, which is read at load time and provides every option that will appear in the rendered field
The Input values key property in the Dynamic Options configuration group, which names the form variable used as the source for the options
The Output mapping expression configured on the surrounding BPMN element, which forwards the variable into the form at task creation time

a.text: Incorrect. Static options items hardcode values onto the schema; they do not let the options follow a process variable like languageData without redeploying the form.

b.text: Incorrect. Default value sets an initial single selection for the field; it does not define the catalogue of options that the field offers to the user.

c.text: Correct. The Input values key property within the Dynamic Options configuration group selects which form variable is used as the source for the options array.

d.text: Incorrect. The BPMN output mapping moves data out of a task, not into the form; option binding is done inside the form field's Dynamic Options group.

explanation: Process-data-driven options use the Input values key property within the Dynamic Options group of the form field to name the variable that supplies the options array.
